message:
fix(fp-io): JSONL reader now unions all keys across rows

read_jsonl_str was only using column names from the first row,
silently dropping keys that appeared only in later rows. For input
like:
  {"a":1,"b":2}
  {"a":3,"c":4}
column "c" would be completely lost.

Fixed by scanning ALL rows to build the union of all keys. Missing
keys in a row produce null values. This matches pandas read_json
behavior with lines=True.

Added regression test with different keys across rows verifying
union column detection and null fill.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
